target.name: cylib
target.founder: Lilian Schwich, Gideon Schwich, Paul Sabarny

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Cylib is a Series A climate and energy company that has raised €140M+ to date, including €55M in Series A equity alongside €26.1M in NRW grants and €63.4M in BMWE grants. The valuation universe comprises nine peers in the climate and energy sector, including one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private companies such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this analysis. The climate and energy sector lacks sufficient comparable companies meeting narrow filtering criteria—zero same-stage peers, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero peers in similar valuation tiers could be identified from the available dataset. While nine total sector peers exist, this set is insufficient for reliable triangulation. Using an all-sector median would introduce substantial error given the heterogeneity of climate and energy business models, capital intensity profiles, and development timelines.

Without triangulated peer analysis, a credible valuation range cannot be derived from the data provided. Key uncertainties limiting the analysis include the absence of stage-appropriate comparables, the capital-intensive nature of the sector masking fundamental differences in burn rate and time-to-revenue across the peer set, and limited transparency on the private peer valuations. Resolution of these data gaps would be necessary before issuing a specific valuation estimate.
